title: rba / rate decision / cash rate | Rate Decision | Monetary Policy Decision | Speeches | RBA
summary: The RBA left the cash rate unchanged at 3.60% on November 4, 2025, as September quarter trimmed mean inflation rose 1.0% (materially above the 0.6% forecast), driven by both temporary factors (travel costs, council rates, fuel) and persistent components (new dwellings, market services). Governor Michele Bullock noted the Board views the September miss as partly temporary but is closely monitoring whether disinflation continues, with the technical assumption of one more rate cut next year now under greater scrutiny.
